findings.evidence: Lines 13-14 say “`K`-points of the base-changed Hopf algebra `K ⊗[k] A`”; the main declaration at lines 101-102 has codomain `WithConv (K ⊗[k] A →ₐ[K] R)` with arbitrary `R`.
findings.fix: Rewrite the sentence to say “`R`-points of the base-changed Hopf algebra over `K`” or “points with values in the `K`-algebra `R`.”
findings.issue: The module docstring calls `K ⊗[k] A →ₐ[K] R` “`K`-points” even though the target is an arbitrary commutative `K`-algebra `R`; these are `R`-points of the base-changed Hopf algebra, not `K`-points unless `R = K`.

summary: The new public API is mostly documented, but one module-level claim uses the wrong functor-of-points terminology and can mislead readers about the target algebra.
